WARNING: These faults are for A2337 (820-02016) MacBook Air 13" M1 ONLY. Do NOT apply A2681 (M2) fault patterns directly. PMU: SERA=Master (U8100), SIMETRA=Slave (U7700).
data_source: Schematic PNGs (pages 22-23, 25, 28, 32, 37-38, 55-57) cross-referenced with saf-diagnostics-2026-03-02.json (22 A2337 devices, 15 with logic board work)

faults.name: No power - CD3217 not negotiating
faults.symptom: Board draws no current or minimal current (<10mA) at 5V. No PPBUS_AON.
faults.probe_point: PPVBUS_USBC0/USBC1 at USB-C connector pins (page 57), UF400/UF500 output
faults.expected_value: 5V on VBUS, CC lines not shorted to ground
faults.fix: Replace UF400 or UF500 (CD3217). If SPI ROM corrupted, reprogram or replace.
faults.board_specific_notes: A2337 has two CD3217 controllers (UF400 ATC0 left rear, UF500 ATC1 left front). No MagSafe alternate power path.

examples: BM 1170: 5V column — 'repair logic board one CD chips and charging port and keyboard change'. Parts: Keyboard, Keyboard Backlight, MacBook Logic Board. Fault keywords: Keyboard, Charging IC.
BM 946: No ammeter — 'repair headphone jack logic board flex and charging port'. Fault keywords: Charging IC.
saf_data.pattern: CD3217/charging port failure accounts for 27% of A2337 logic board repairs. Often discovered at 5V ammeter stage when board draws nothing.

faults.name: No power - Charger IC fault
faults.symptom: 5V present at VBUS but no PPBUS_AON. Board draws 50-200mA at 5V but does not boot.
faults.probe_point: PPBUS_AON bulk capacitor near U5200 output, CZ243/CZ245 (page 23)
causes: Battery SYS_DETECT not triggered — J5100 gasket, Q5150 (page 22)
faults.fix: If SYS_DETECT missing: check J5100 connector and gasket circuit. If charger IC dead: replace U5200.
faults.board_specific_notes: At 5V without battery, PPBUS_AON may not establish on A2337. Battery or 20V DCPS needed for full test. Gate drives CHGR_GATE_G1-G7 visible on page 23.

saf_data.pattern: Charging IC/port is the #2 component failure (27%) on A2337 after keyboard. Often co-occurs with other damage (liquid, keyboard).

faults.name: No power - PP3V8_AON short
faults.symptom: PPBUS_AON present but PP3V8_AON missing or low. High current draw on PPBUS_AON.
faults.probe_point: PP3V8_AON bulk capacitors B5700/CS751/CS752 (page 25), PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN distribution (page 79)
faults.expected_value: 3.8V on PP3V8_AON, normal diode mode readings (>0.15V)
causes: Short circuit on PP3V8_AON VDDMAIN branch capacitors
faults.fix: Diode mode on PP3V8_AON caps. Thermal imaging at low voltage. Replace shorted capacitor or IC. Common: capacitor failure on VDDMAIN branch.
faults.board_specific_notes: A2337 PP3V8_AON distribution is on pages 25-27 and 79. VDDMAIN and VODMAN are separate branches. U5700 is 30A design at 1MHz CCMIA.

examples: BM 1067: 20V/1.4A — 'clan logic board liquid damage and repair liquid damage parts macbook working fine'. Liquid damage on PP3V8_AON area is a common M1 fault pattern.
saf_data.pattern: Liquid damage cases on A2337 often affect PP3V8_AON distribution. 20V/1.4A reading suggests partial power path — board gets past charger but stalls at SVR/PMU level.

faults.name: No power - U5700 SVR AON failure
faults.symptom: PPBUS_AON present, PPVAON_PWR_EN asserted, but PP3V8_AON missing. No short detected.
faults.probe_point: U5700 output capacitor, PPVAON_PWR_EN (page 25, delayed by D5340/D5345), PPVAON_FAULT_L
faults.expected_value: 3.8V on PP3V8_AON output, PPVAON_FAULT_L high (no fault)
causes: U5700 SVR AON controller IC failure
faults.fix: Replace U5700 SVR AON controller. Check phase outputs on page 26.
faults.board_specific_notes: U5700 is a 30A multi-phase buck at 1MHz. Phase drivers on page 26. Enable signal is delayed by D5340/D5345 from SYS_DETECT.

faults.name: No boot - PMU not starting
faults.symptom: PP3V8_AON present at 3.8V but PMU LDO outputs missing. No PP5V_S2 or PP3V3_S2.

dcps_signature.20V: 0.3-0.5A (stuck in AON state, PMU not cascading)

faults.probe_point: SERA LDO9 output cap (~1.8V, page 34), SIMETRA LDO9 output cap (~1.8V, page 29), PMU_ACTIVE_READY signal
faults.expected_value: SERA LDO9=~1.8V, SIMETRA LDO9=~1.8V, PMU_ACTIVE_READY asserted
causes: Liquid damage on PMU BGA pads
faults.fix: Replace failed PMU IC: U8100 (SERA/Master) or U7700 (SIMETRA/Slave). If single LDO missing, find downstream short first.
faults.board_specific_notes: A2337 uses SERA (Master, U8100, pages 32-35) and SIMETRA (Slave, U7700, pages 28-31) PMU naming.

saf_data.pattern: PMU-level faults typically present as 20V/0.3-0.5A (board stuck in AON state). BM 1111 at 20V/0.5A may represent a PMU-adjacent fault — keyboard + battery replacement alongside low current reading.

faults.name: No boot - PP5V_S2 missing
faults.symptom: PMU rails present but PP5V_S2 missing. Board partially powers on but does not boot.
faults.probe_point: UC300 (SMC322) output capacitor (page 38), probe points PPC10-PPC15 (page 37), P5VS2_PWR_EN enable signal (page 37)
causes: P5VS2_PWR_EN not asserted by PMU GPIO
faults.fix: If P5VS2_PWR_EN missing: trace PMU GPIO. If output shorted: find and fix short. If UC300 dead: replace UC300.
faults.board_specific_notes: A2337 uses UC300 (SMC322, NOT UC320) for PP5V_S2. Inductor LC320 and sense XMC320. Probe points PPC10-15 on page 37.

faults.name: No boot - PP3V3_S2 missing
faults.symptom: PMU rails present but PP3V3_S2 missing.
faults.probe_point: UC710 output capacitor (page 39), probe points PPC210-PPC215 (page 37), P3V3S2_PWR_EN enable signal (page 37)
causes: P3V3S2_PWR_EN gating logic fault (page 37) — NOTE: Per Sera PMU OFF, GPIO11 is OFF when System is OFF
faults.fix: Fix P3V3S2_PWR_EN path or replace UC710.
faults.board_specific_notes: A2337 uses UC710 for PP3V3_S2. XMC710/XMC711 are external sense components. Probe points PPC210-215 on page 37. Discharge circuit JC211 on page 37.

faults.name: USB-C port not working (one port)
faults.symptom: One USB-C port does not charge or pass data. Other port works fine.
faults.probe_point: CC1/CC2 lines on affected port (page 55/56), VBUS diode mode, SBU1/SBU2
faults.expected_value: CC lines not shorted, VBUS not dead shorted, SBU lines normal
causes: CD3217 port controller fault on affected port
faults.fix: Replace connector if physical damage. Replace CD3217 (UF400 or UF500) if controller fault.
faults.board_specific_notes: A2337 port 0 (ATC0, UF400) = left rear. Port 1 (ATC1, UF500) = left front. Both ports left side only. No MagSafe backup.

saf_data.pattern: CD3217 failure is common on A2337. SAF data shows charging port repair in multiple cases. Single-port failure may present as 'charges on one side only'.

faults.name: No display - backlight fault
faults.symptom: Board boots (chime, fan) but no display. External display may work.
faults.probe_point: DP600 (BEN) input/output (page 69), display connector (page 67), UP700/UP705/UP715 outputs (page 68)
faults.expected_value: PPBUS_AONSW_LCD present, backlight output voltage present, eDP data active
faults.fix: Replace DP600 (BEN), display connector, or display cable. If UP700/UP705 sequencer fault: replace sequencer IC.
faults.board_specific_notes: A2337 uses DP600 for backlight (not U6560 as on A2681). SECDIS MIPI mux on page 66.

faults.name: Keyboard not working
faults.symptom: Board boots and works but keyboard unresponsive or partially working.
faults.probe_point: BEN controller (page 69-70), keyboard matrix (page 70), keyboard connector
causes: Liquid damage on keyboard connector area
faults.fix: Replace keyboard assembly. Often requires keyboard backlight replacement too (shared A2179/A2337 part).
faults.board_specific_notes: Keyboard and keyboard backlight use shared A2179/A2337 parts.

examples: BM 1149: 20V/0.8A — 'Post repair all good, we repair new keyboard'
BM 1226: 20V/2.5A — 'we repair new keyboard and keyboard back light'
BM 1451: 20V/2.0A — 'we repair new keyboard, keyboard back light we make track pad alignment (has been repair keyboard they damage all keyboard housing rebate)'
BM 1450: 20V/2.0A — 'we repair new keyboard, keyboard back light, charging port and Bezel and black screen plastic, clan touch id'
saf_data.pattern: Keyboard is the DOMINANT fault on A2337 — 60% of all logic board cases. Often co-occurs with keyboard backlight, charging port, and/or trackpad damage. Multiple SAF cases show keyboard + backlight as a paired repair.

faults.name: Audio IC failure
faults.symptom: Board boots normally but no audio from speakers. Headphone jack may also be affected.
faults.probe_point: Audio level shifters (page 73), amplifiers (page 74), audio connectors (page 75)
faults.expected_value: Audio output present from speakers and headphone jack
causes: Audio IC failure on logic board
Liquid damage on audio circuit area
faults.board_specific_notes: Audio IC repair requires board-level soldering expertise.

examples: BM 1223: 5V column — 'repair audio logic board' then 'repair logic board sound ic and pull battery'. Fault keywords: Keyboard, Audio IC, Battery.
BM 1313: 20V/2.5A — 'repair logic board and audio logic board repair and we change top case'. Fault keywords: Audio IC.
saf_data.pattern: Audio IC failure on A2337 requires board-level soldering. SAF shows it sometimes co-occurs with other faults (keyboard, battery). 2 of 15 logic board cases (13%).
